post.description: Generates a model response for the given conversation. This endpoint is fully compatible with the OpenAI Chat Completions API.

x-codeSamples.label: Python (OpenAI SDK)
x-codeSamples.source:
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key="your-api-key",
    base_url="https://api.subconscious.dev/v1",
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="subconscious/tim-qwen3.6-27b",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"}],
)

print(response.choices[0].message.content)

x-codeSamples.label: Node.js (OpenAI SDK)
cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl https://api.subconscious.dev/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "subconscious/tim-qwen3.6-27b",
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"}]
  }'

model.description: The model to use for completion.

messages.description: The conversation history. Each message has a role and content.

role.description: The role of the message author.

example.content: What is the capital of France?

stream.description: If true, responses are streamed as Server-Sent Events. Each event is a ChatCompletionChunk object. The stream ends with `data: [DONE]`.

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate in the response.

max_completion_tokens.description: An alternative to max_tokens. Maximum number of tokens to generate.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. Lower values make output more focused and deterministic, while higher values make it more creative.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Only considers tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds this threshold.

frequency_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on their frequency in the text so far.

presence_penalty.description: Penalizes tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far.

stop.description: Up to 4 sequences where the model will stop generating.

response_format.description: Constrains the response format. Use `{"type": "json_object"}` for valid JSON or `{"type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...}}` for schema-constrained output.

json_schema.description: The JSON schema to constrain the output to. Required when type is json_schema.

include_usage.description: If true, include token usage in the final streamed chunk.

chat_template_kwargs.description: Subconscious extension. Controls model-specific features like thinking mode.

enable_thinking.description: If true, enables step-by-step reasoning. The model will output its reasoning in <think> tags before the final answer.

id.description: A unique identifier for the completion.

created.description: Unix timestamp of when the completion was created.

model.description: The model used for the completion.

finish_reason.description: The reason the model stopped generating.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the prompt.

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the generated completion.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid parameters or unsupported options

401.description: Unauthorized because of a missing or invalid API key

429.description: Rate limited due to too many requests or tokens per minute

post.description: Generates a model response for the given conversation. This endpoint is fully compatible with the Anthropic Messages API, so you can use the Anthropic SDK by pointing its base URL at Subconscious.

x-codeSamples.label: Python (Anthropic SDK)
x-codeSamples.source:
from anthropic import Anthropic

client = Anthropic(
    auth_token="your-api-key",
    base_url="https://api.subconscious.dev",
)

message = client.messages.create(
    model="subconscious/tim-qwen3.6-27b",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"}],
)

print(message.content[0].text)

x-codeSamples.label: Node.js (Anthropic SDK)
cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl https://api.subconscious.dev/v1/messages \
  -H "x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01" \
  -H "content-type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "model": "subconscious/tim-qwen3.6-27b",
    "max_tokens": 1024,
    "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is the capital of France?"}]
  }'

model.description: The model to use for completion.

messages.description: The conversation history. Each message has a role and content. Content is either a string or an array of content blocks (text, image, tool_use, tool_result, thinking).

role.description: The role of the message author. System prompts are passed via the top-level `system` field, not as a message.

items.description: A content block. `type` selects the block shape: `text` ({text}), `image` ({source}), `tool_use` ({id, name, input}), `tool_result` ({tool_use_id, content, is_error}), `thinking` ({thinking}).

source.description: Image source. Either base64 ({type:"base64", media_type, data}) or url ({type:"url", url}).

tool_use_id.description: tool_result block: the id of the tool_use it answers.

content.description: tool_result block content (string or array of blocks).

is_error.description: tool_result block: whether the tool call errored.

example.content: What is the capital of France?

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping. Required.

system.description: A system prompt: a string or an array of text content blocks.

stream.description: If true, responses are streamed as Server-Sent Events using the Anthropic event protocol (`message_start`, `content_block_start`, `content_block_delta`, `content_block_stop`, `message_delta`, `message_stop`).

temperature.description: Amount of randomness injected into the response.

top_k.description: Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token.

stop_sequences.description: Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating.

tools.description: Definitions of tools the model may use.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input.

tool_choice.description: How the model should use the provided tools.

name.description: Required when type is `tool`: the tool the model must use.

thinking.description: Enable extended thinking. When enabled, the response includes `thinking` content blocks before the final `text` block.

metadata.description: An object describing metadata about the request.

chat_template_kwargs.description: Subconscious extension. Controls model-specific features such as auto-compaction (Subconscious Cache).

enable_auto_compaction.description: If false, disables automatic context compaction so you can control Subconscious Cache manually.

id.description: A unique identifier for the message.

content.description: An array of content blocks. Text blocks ({type:"text", text}), thinking blocks ({type:"thinking", thinking, signature}), and tool_use blocks ({type:"tool_use", id, name, input}).

stop_reason.description: The reason the model stopped generating.

stop_sequence.description: The custom stop sequence that was generated, if any.

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Number of input tokens read from cache.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid parameters or unsupported options

401.description: Unauthorized because of a missing or invalid API key

429.description: Rate limited due to too many requests or tokens per minute

bearerAuth.description: API key from your Subconscious dashboard

apiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key from your Subconscious dashboard. Accepted by the Anthropic Messages endpoints in place of the Authorization header, for use with the Anthropic SDK.
